summary_one_line: Input must begin with the 4-byte Mach-O magic cf fa ed fe so magic equals MH_MAGIC/MH_MAGIC_64/FAT_CIGAM.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The first 4 bytes of the input must equal the Mach-O magic word cf fa ed fe at offset 0x00-0x03 so that the read magic compares equal to one of MH_MAGIC / MH_MAGIC_64 / FAT_CIGAM at macho.cc:618, taking the true branch into the MachOObjectFile constructor.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every cmplog winner-resolving seed begins with the exact bytes cf fa ed fe at offsets 0x00-0x03, which is the little-endian Mach-O magic; the per-offset byte sets show these four offsets are fully concentrated to that value across all 10 winner seeds.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser-blocking seeds begin with 00 61 73 6d ('\0asm', the WebAssembly magic), so magic never matches any Mach-O constant and execution falls through to the nullptr return at line 623, exercising the false branch.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the logged CMP operands from the magic==MH_MAGIC comparison and splices the constant magic value into the input at the matching offset; naive has no CMP introspection and cannot synthesize the exact 4-byte magic, so it never crosses the gate.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch at macho.cc:618 fires the true side only when magic equals one of three Mach-O magic constants.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: if (magic == MH_MAGIC || magic == MH_MAGIC_64 || magic == FAT_CIGAM) {

evidence_trail.claim: All winner-resolving seeds carry the Mach-O magic cf fa ed fe in their first four bytes.
The winning seed bytes literally start with cf fa ed fe.
The loser-blocking seeds start with the WebAssembly magic \0asm and not a Mach-O magic.
Loser inputs fall through TryOpenMachOFile to the nullptr return because magic does not match.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 618  T=96 F=0  T=0 F=120  if (magic == MH_MAGIC || magic == MH_MAGIC_64 || magic ==...  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which scans the input for an operand and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.
**Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 133 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 492ed499061ee540`; the ancestor chain contains one I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 3 (ancestor a24245b7a5bd58f1), the under-build signal of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find exclusive to cmplog/vpc.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on a 4-byte exact-equality magic where naive resolves the gate as often as cmplog (no cmplog>naive divergence), showing the magic byte is reachable by plain havoc rather than requiring I2S substitution.

weakest_evidence_point: The dash-row I2S signal is a lower-bound floor at depth 3 of one seed's chain; it confirms some I2S contribution but does not prove the specific magic-byte splice was the I2S edit rather than a later havoc refinement.
